utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: patient mrn : abc123 . date of birth : january 1st , 1960 . patient is a 60 year old , right-handed male , referred today for evaluation of numbness and tingling .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , mr. cook . how are you doing this morning ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , dr. martinez . this weekend was a bit rough . i've had this numbness and tingling for a few weeks now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so it's in both of your hands ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , ever since my car wreck last december when i broke them ... well , once they healed , i mean , i noticed it more , the tingling and the numbness .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you still wear the splint only on the right wrist ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i also wear the left sometimes . the right is just worse , but the splint helps . both have really gotten worse over the last month . when i broke them , my right wrist was definitely in worse shape . i've also been having some s- mild stiffness in my neck too . i hit my head during the accident , and they told me it was a contusion .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and where would you say the tingling and numbness is ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: on this side , it goes from here and here up to about here . on the other side , it's from here to here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . so on the right side , you're pointing from the middle and ring finger , all the way up the forearm . and on the left , the symptoms extend from the pinky and ring finger . and is there any pain or just tingling and numbness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: eh , it's mainly just sore . sometimes they throb , but overall , they feel weaker too , especially when i'm using them . the tingling happens more when they hurt , but when it goes away , the tingling is dull .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and when do you feel these symptoms ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , really , i guess , just more so at night , and they come and go throughout the day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do you experience any loss of sensation in your hands or fingers ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: like , feeling something ? no , not that i can really think of . though , in general , they may be less sensitive than they should be , but i can still feel . they also gave me some nerve pain medicine , but i have n't been taking it like i should .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and are you still on the coreg for hypertension and the lexapro for anxiety ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i'm still taking those . i had prediabetes too , but i got it under control by changing my diet .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and no history of thyroid issues , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , thank you , mr. cook . okay , first , we'll go over your review of systems . correct me if i'm wrong in any of this .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: constitutional , you have fatigue and weight gain ; gi , you have excessive appetite and bloating ; eyes have floaters ; cardiovascular shows shortness of breath and leg swelling ; endocrine , you're always hungry ; skin , you have some nail bed changes , dryness , itching ; musculoskeletal , you have gout and muscle pain . and the remainder of the review of systems is negative .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep , that's all correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , we're going to go ahead and do the neurologic examination . let's look at the right hand first . can you feel this ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but a little less on these two fingers .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sensation is normal , except pin prick sensation diminished in third and fourth fingers on the right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about on the left ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , yeah , but these two a little less .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sensation is decreased in the fourth and fifth fingers on the left

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , let's test your reflexes now . reflexes are 1+ and symmetric ; 2+ knees ; toes are downgoing bilaterally .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , i'm going to tap on your right wrist . do you feel anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it feels like a zing up my wrist and something into my elbow too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about the left side ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , same on that side too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: tinel sign positive at both wrists and both elbows . rest of exam is normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so what i am seeing today is bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome or , potentially , this could also be bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome . however , your symptom presentation suggests car- bilateral carpal syndrome , as the nerve in your wrists are aggravated by your past trauma . this is causing the pain and tingling you were describing to me .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , so how do we make it better ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: first , i want to determine exactly where the nerve irritation is coming from . we'll need to schedule you for a nerve test , which is called an emg test . they will measure the responsiveness of your nerves to truly pinpoint where you are having your issues .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds fine to me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . after the tests are complete , you can return here for a follow-up visit . if you're still having symptoms , we can consider a referral to our hand clinic , possibly for injections or carpal tunnel release se- surgery . you could also refer ... we could also refer you to an orthopedist for the same treatment .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good , doc .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , keep wearing your splints , and i will see you after those studies .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 60-year-old male presenting for evaluation of numbness and tingling in his hands.
The patient reports numbness and tingling in his hands for a few weeks.
The doctor asks if the symptoms are present in both hands.
The patient reports symptoms began after a motor vehicle accident in December 2019.
The doctor asks if the patient is still wearing a splint only on the right wrist.
The patient wears splints on both wrists, reports worsening symptoms over the last month, and mentions neck stiffness and a head contusion from the accident.
The doctor asks for the specific location of the numbness and tingling.
The doctor confirms symptoms involve the middle and ring fingers on the right and the pinky and ring fingers on the left.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences pain or only numbness and tingling.
The patient reports soreness, throbbing, weakness, and dull tingling.
The doctor asks when the symptoms occur.
The patient reports symptoms occur mostly at night and come and go throughout the day.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced any loss of sensation.
The patient denies complete loss of sensation but notes decreased sensitivity and reports not taking prescribed nerve pain medication.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking Coreg and Lexapro.
The patient confirms taking Coreg and Lexapro and reports a history of prediabetes controlled by diet.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of thyroid issues.
Review of systems
The doctor reviews systems including fatigue, weight gain, floaters, shortness of breath, edema, appetite, bloating, hunger, skin changes, gout, and muscle pain.
Neurological examination
Pinprick sensation is diminished in the 3rd and 4th fingers on the right hand.
Sensation is decreased in the 4th and 5th fingers on the left hand.
Reflexes are 1+ and symmetric, 2+ at knees, and toes are downgoing bilaterally.
Tinel's sign is positive at both wrists and elbows.
The doctor assesses the patient with bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome, potentially bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome.
The patient asks about treatment options.
The doctor plans to order an EMG test to determine the source of nerve irritation.
The doctor plans a follow-up visit after EMG testing and potential referral to a hand clinic or orthopedist.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue wearing splints.

case_description: numbness and tingling of hands; none
